description_long: This is a compact mechanical keyboard with a white keycap set arranged in a dense, space-saving layout. The keyboard features a visible orange-brown circuit board base, with some exposed electronic components and green switches visible along the top edge. The keycaps are labeled in English and show signs of wear and discoloration, indicating frequent use. The keyboard lacks a dedicated numpad, suggesting a tenkeyless or 65% layout, and there is no visible branding on the surface. The rear of the keyboard reveals the printed circuit board with solder points and traces, while the sides show the profile of the keycaps and the exposed edge of the PCB. The overall construction appears to be primarily plastic and PCB, with some metal components likely present internally for switch mechanisms.
description: A compact, white mechanical keyboard with visible circuit board and worn keycaps, lacking a numpad and branding. The keyboard shows exposed electronics and a dense key layout.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full key layout, white keycaps with English labels, and the orange-brown PCB base.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the profile of the keycaps, exposed green switches, and the edge of the PCB.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the green printed circuit board with solder points and the side profile of the keycaps.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other edge of the keyboard, keycap profile, and exposed PCB.
